text: On Juniper Junos, when a BGP neighbor shows state Idle with error tcp_connect_failed, begin with underlay verification. Use 'ping <peer_ip> routing-instance <vrf> count 5' to test reachability. Check 'show route <peer_ip>' to verify a route to the peer exists. If the route exists and ping succeeds, verify the BGP configuration: 'show bgp neighbor <ip>' shows the configured local-address, peer-as, and authentication. Ensure local-address matches an interface address the peer is configured to accept. Check that TCP port 179 is not blocked by a firewall filter on the lo0 or physical interface. Use 'show bgp neighbor <ip> | match state' to see the exact FSM state and error codes.

On Juniper Junos, use 'clear bgp neighbor <ip> soft' to perform an inbound soft reset — this sends a route-refresh message asking the peer to re-advertise its routes without tearing the session. Use 'clear bgp neighbor <ip> graceful' for a graceful restart which preserves forwarding state during reconvergence. For outbound policy changes, use 'clear bgp neighbor <ip> export' to re-evaluate and re-advertise routes to the peer under the new export policy. A hard reset with 'clear bgp neighbor <ip>' should only be used when the session is already down or when the operator has confirmed the impact. Always prefer soft operations during business hours. After a soft reset, verify prefix counts recover with 'show bgp neighbor <ip> | match received'.

section_title: Zero Prefixes Received on Established Session
text: When a Junos BGP session is Established but receives zero prefixes, start with 'show bgp neighbor <ip> | match received' to confirm prefix counts. Then check the sender side: 'show route advertising-protocol bgp <peer_ip>' on the peer shows what routes are being advertised. If the peer advertises routes but the receiver sees none, check export policies on the sender and import policies on the receiver. On Junos, import policies filter received routes before installing them in inet.0. Use 'show route receive-protocol bgp <peer_ip>' to see all routes received before import policy is applied — if this shows routes but 'show route' does not, the import policy is filtering them. Check 'show policy <policy_name>' to inspect the policy logic.

section_title: Route Reflector Failure on Junos Fabrics
text: On Juniper Junos fabrics with route reflectors, a route reflector failure causes all iBGP client sessions to drop. When multiple leafs show Idle or Active state toward the same RR cluster, treat this as a single incident. On Junos, 'show bgp summary' on the route reflector shows all client sessions and their state. The RR logs (show log messages | match bgp) show session establishment and teardown events. If the RR is reachable but sessions are not reconverging, check 'show bgp group' to verify the cluster-id is correctly configured and the group type is internal. Clients should have the RR configured as a single neighbor — check 'show bgp neighbor <rr_ip> | match cluster'. Do not reset individual client sessions until the RR health is confirmed.

Essential Juniper Junos BGP verification commands: 'show bgp summary' shows all neighbors, state, and prefix counts. 'show bgp neighbor <ip>' shows full session detail including FSM state, error codes, timers, and capabilities. 'show route advertising-protocol bgp <ip>' shows routes being advertised to the peer. 'show route receive-protocol bgp <ip>' shows all routes received from the peer before import policy. 'show route protocol bgp' shows BGP routes in the routing table after import policy. 'show bgp group' shows peer groups and their configuration. For EVPN: 'show bgp l2vpn evpn summary'. For detailed troubleshooting: 'show bgp neighbor <ip> extensive' includes packet statistics and error history.
